channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: 'Hormuz will not return to previous state', Iran
item.description: Donald Trump said a shooting incident will not deter him from winning in Iran. He made the statement amid ceasefire talks. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Shooting incident won't deter me from win in Iran war, says Trump The Times of India.

item.title: Marathon-Kenya's Sawe becomes first man to run marathon under two hours to win in London
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrived in the US for a four-day state visit, amid a tense transatlantic relationship. The royal couple's visit comes as the US and UK face disagreements under President Trump.

item.title: Philippines says two US citizens among 19 killed in raid
item.description: Two US citizens, Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem, were killed in a raid in Negros Occidental province. The two Americans, identified as Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem, arrived in Negros Occidental province about a month before they were killed, officials said in a statement late on Saturday (Apr 25).

item.title: Iranian Negotiators Set to Return to Pakistan to Try to Revive Truce Talks
item.description: Iranian negotiators are set to return to Pakistan to revive truce talks. The move follows President Trump's decision to call off a trip by his top advisers, leaving the outcome of the negotiations uncertain.

item.title: Islamabad Reopens After U.S.-Iran Talks Fail to Materialize
item.description: Islamabad reopened after US-Iran talks failed to materialize. The city was locked down in anticipation of the talks, but officials now say they will not proceed. Officials had locked the city down, anticipating talks between U. S.

item.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old tutor and computer programmer, has been accused of shooting at the White House correspondents' dinner. Allen, a 2017 mechanical engineering graduate from Caltech, is facing charges.

item.title: Trump WH correspondents' dinner shooting LIVE: Accused is a tutor, computer programmer from
item.description: Pakistan reopened Islamabad after US-Iran talks did not happen. The city was locked down in anticipation of the talks, but officials now say they will not proceed, leaving business owners frustrated.

item.title: Yemen’s landmine crisis endures despite truce and de-mining efforts
item.description: Landmines in Yemen continue to kill and maim despite ceasefire efforts. De-mining initiatives are ongoing. Landmines in Yemen continue to kill and maim, even amid ceasefire efforts and ongoing de-mining initiatives.

item.title: "Army Doesn't Get Business": US Soldier Accused Of Betting On Maduro Operation
item.description: Gannon Ken Van Dyke, a US soldier, was part of the operation to capture Maduro in January. He is accused of betting on the operation. Gannon Ken Van Dyke was part of the operation to capture Maduro in January.

item.title: Swiss International says five injured after flight aborts takeoff in India
item.description: Swiss International flight aborts takeoff in India, five injured. The incident occurred during takeoff. Swiss International says five injured after flight aborts takeoff in India.

item.title: Trump Was Going To "Rip It" With Media, Then Literal Shots Were Fired
item.description: Trump entered the Hilton with an animus towards the media. Shots were fired during the incident. Trump entered the Hilton with the weight of an animus with the media seen throughout his second term.

item.title: U.S. Mint Buys Drug Cartel Gold and Sells It as ‘American’
item.description: The US Mint buys gold from various sources, including drug cartels, and sells it as American. As prices for the precious metal soar, the industry’s guardrails have broken down. U. S.

item.title: Israel’s President, Putting Off Decision on Pardon for Netanyahu, Will Push for Plea Deal
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog will not pardon Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. He will seek a plea deal instead. President Isaac Herzog of Israel has decided not to issue a pardon to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in his corruption case at this time, and instead will seek mediation, officials say.

item.title: Oman's Sultan meets Iran's Araghchi to discuss regional situation
item.description: Oman's Sultan met Iran's Araghchi to discuss the regional situation. The meeting occurred amid US-Iran tensions. U. S. President Trump cancels Pakistan talks trip and says Iran war on hold; U. S.

item.title: Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
item.description: Armed groups launched coordinated attacks across Mali, with clashes reported in the centre and north. Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

item.title: What is the White House Correspondents’ Dinner?
item.description: The White House Correspondents’ Dinner is an annual event. What is the White House Correspondents’ Dinner?

item.title: Who is Cole Tomas Allen, suspect apprehended in connection to shooting in Washington
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of Torrance, California, was identified as the shooting suspect. described by Trump as a “sick person”. was identified as Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of Torrance, California.

item.title: China launches Pakistani satellite
item.description: China launched the Pakistani satellite PRSC-EO3 using a Long March-6 carrier rocket. The satellite named PRSC-EO3 was lifted off at 8:15 p. m. (Beijing Time) by a Long March-6 carrier rocket and successfully entered its planned orbit.

item.title: Who is Cole Allen, the suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting?
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old California resident, was identified as the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect. The suspect arrested in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting has been identified as Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old California resident.

item.title: U.S. lawmakers say violence has no place in the country
item.description: Democrat leader Ro Khanna said political violence is unacceptable in the US after a Secret Service agent was shot. My thoughts are with the brave Secret Service agent who was shot.
